growth_records.cite: Savin et al. In-host evolution of Yersinia enterocolitica during a chronic human infection. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60782-6. [PMC12218051]
growth_records.snippet: A substantial increase in mid-exponential phase doubling time at 37 °C was measured, from 49 and 44 min for Ye.1 and Ye.2 to 5h38 and 4h46 for Ye.3 and Ye.4, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (49 min = 0.8167 h) explicitly reported for mid-exponential growth in LB at 37C, 200 rpm. growth_rate_per_h = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/0.8167 = 0.8486 h^-1. Oxygen state (aerobic) inferred from 200 rpm shaking flask culture, not explicitly labeled 'aerobic' in text. Full LB recipe (g/L) not given in this paper for this experiment; batch flask growth curves (250 mL LB, 2.5 mL preculture inoculum) per Methods.

growth_records.cite: Savin et al. In-host evolution of Yersinia enterocolitica during a chronic human infection. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60782-6. [PMC12218051]
growth_records.snippet: A substantial increase in mid-exponential phase doubling time at 37 °C was measured, from 49 and 44 min for Ye.1 and Ye.2 to 5h38 and 4h46 for Ye.3 and Ye.4, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (44 min = 0.7333 h) explicitly reported for mid-exponential growth in LB at 37C, 200 rpm. growth_rate_per_h = ln2/0.7333 = 0.9452 h^-1. Oxygen state inferred from shaking flask culture.

growth_records.strain: Ye.3 (bioserotype 4/O:3, late clinical isolate, nalidixic-acid resistant, gyrA-mutated)
growth_records.cite: Savin et al. In-host evolution of Yersinia enterocolitica during a chronic human infection. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60782-6. [PMC12218051]
growth_records.snippet: A substantial increase in mid-exponential phase doubling time at 37 °C was measured, from 49 and 44 min for Ye.1 and Ye.2 to 5h38 and 4h46 for Ye.3 and Ye.4, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (5h38min = 5.6333 h) explicitly reported for mid-exponential growth in LB at 37C, 200 rpm (severe growth defect, antibiotic-tolerance phenotype). growth_rate_per_h = ln2/5.6333 = 0.1231 h^-1. Oxygen state inferred from shaking flask culture.

growth_records.cite: Savin et al. In-host evolution of Yersinia enterocolitica during a chronic human infection. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60782-6. [PMC12218051]
growth_records.snippet: A substantial increase in mid-exponential phase doubling time at 37 °C was measured, from 49 and 44 min for Ye.1 and Ye.2 to 5h38 and 4h46 for Ye.3 and Ye.4, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (4h46min = 4.7667 h) explicitly reported for mid-exponential growth in LB at 37C, 200 rpm (severe growth defect). growth_rate_per_h = ln2/4.7667 = 0.1454 h^-1. Oxygen state inferred from shaking flask culture.

growth_records.cite: Savin et al. In-host evolution of Yersinia enterocolitica during a chronic human infection. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60782-6. [PMC12218051]
growth_records.snippet: Doubling times at 28 °C were slightly longer, with 57 min for Ye.1 and Ye.2, 6h49 for Ye.3 and 5h28 for Ye.4 (Supplementary Fig. 1).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (57 min = 0.95 h) reported for mid-exponential growth in LB at 28C, 200 rpm. growth_rate_per_h = ln2/0.95 = 0.7296 h^-1.

growth_records.cite: Savin et al. In-host evolution of Yersinia enterocolitica during a chronic human infection. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60782-6. [PMC12218051]
growth_records.snippet: Doubling times at 28 °C were slightly longer, with 57 min for Ye.1 and Ye.2, 6h49 for Ye.3 and 5h28 for Ye.4 (Supplementary Fig. 1).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (6h49min = 6.8167 h) reported for mid-exponential growth in LB at 28C, 200 rpm. growth_rate_per_h = ln2/6.8167 = 0.1017 h^-1.

growth_records.cite: Savin et al. In-host evolution of Yersinia enterocolitica during a chronic human infection. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60782-6. [PMC12218051]
growth_records.snippet: Doubling times at 28 °C were slightly longer, with 57 min for Ye.1 and Ye.2, 6h49 for Ye.3 and 5h28 for Ye.4 (Supplementary Fig. 1).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (5h28min = 5.4667 h) reported for mid-exponential growth in LB at 28C, 200 rpm. growth_rate_per_h = ln2/5.4667 = 0.1268 h^-1.
